question: What is the difference between liposomes and solid lipid nanoparticles (SLNs)?
options: Liposomes have aqueous cores, SLNs have solid lipid matrices
Liposomes are solid, SLNs are liquid
Liposomes are larger, SLNs are smaller
explanation: Liposomes consist of lipid bilayers surrounding aqueous cores, while SLNs are composed of solid lipid matrices. SLNs offer better stability and controlled release.
